mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and business demand, forcing equity valuations lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive 60-day forward returns in over 60 percent of cases, or interaction term coefficient loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in quarterly regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during a monetary easing cycle trigger flight-to-quality flows into equities as investors anticipate aggressive central bank liquidity support, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during industrial production declines under easing conditions are not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.5), or the signal reverses direction in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for payroll growth remains below 0.01 across all 1-year rolling windows, or 5-day forward returns show zero predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 240.0 indicates accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in tighter future monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.0, or inflation threshold loses predictive accuracy (AUC < 0.55) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales signals weakening consumer momentum relative to aggregate output, prompting equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction term between GDP and retail sales shows positive or null coefficients in monthly return regressions, or 20-day forward returns during divergence periods exceed baseline by more than 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a high unemployment regime confirms a broad economic contraction, eliminating policy offset expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during industrial production declines in high unemployment periods are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 2 percent annually.
